name: Papiamentu (Papiamento)

ancestors.notes: Portuguese superstrate: earliest Iberian lexifier; the Portuguese origin hypothesis (Goodman, Maurer) holds that Papiamentu originated from a Portuguese-based creole spoken by Sephardic Jews and Portuguese traders from the early 17th century.

Dutch superstrate: Netherlands ruled Curaçao from 1634; Dutch colonial administration contributed vocabulary, and Dutch is still a co-official language. Official Curaçao orthography (2009) reflects phonemic principles distinct from Dutch etymology.

notes: Papiamentu (Curaçao/Bonaire) / Papiamento (Aruba). Iberian-based creole; official language of Curaçao, Aruba, and Bonaire. ~330,000 speakers. Two spelling conventions: Curaçao/Bonaire use phonemic orthography (papi-a-men-tu), Aruba uses etymological spelling closer to Spanish/Portuguese. KEY FEATURES: (1) FIVE-VOWEL SYSTEM: a/e/i/o/u; e and o vary [e]~[ɛ] and [o]~[ɔ]. (2) AFFRICATES: ch=[tʃ], dj=[dʒ]. (3) SIBILANTS: sh=[ʃ], zh=[ʒ], s=[s], z=[z]. (4) PALATAL NASAL: ñ=[ɲ] (from Spanish/Portuguese). (5) LL: renders [j] (from Spanish lateral, reanalysed as glide). (6) DIPHTHONGS: ai/ei/oi/au are phonemic. (7) STRESS: penultimate by default; stress-timed rhythm. One of the best-studied Atlantic creoles with the highest number of L1 speakers per capita in the Caribbean. This spec models the CURAÇAO/BONAIRE phonemic orthography (FPI 2009: ⟨k⟩ not ⟨c⟩, acute ⟨á é í ó ú⟩ for stress mapped to base vowels), not the Aruban etymological spelling.

sources.author: Kouwenberg, S. & Murray, E.
sources.title: Papiamentu
sources.publisher: Languages of the World/Materials 83, LINCOM Europa
sources.pages: Phonology ch.: five-vowel system with [e]~[ɛ], [o]~[ɔ] alternations; affricates [tʃ dʒ]; sibilants [s z ʃ ʒ]; coda /s/ palatalisation to [ʃ] before consonants and word-finally; preverbal TMA markers ta (imperfective), a (perfective), lo (future).
sources.notes: Comprehensive phonological and morphological description; source for the vowel and sibilant inventory and the coda-/s/ rule.

sources.author: Römer, Raúl G.
sources.title: Studies in Papiamentu tonology
sources.publisher: Caribbean Culture Studies 5, University of Amsterdam
sources.pages: Lexical tone/stress; interaction of the acute-accent orthography (muhé, kachó, akí) with stressed syllables.
sources.notes: Grounds the acute-accented-vowel graphemes as stress marks over the base five vowels.

sources.author: Fundashon pa Planifikashon di Idioma (FPI)
sources.title: Ortografia di papiamentu (revised Curaçao/Bonaire spelling)
sources.publisher: Fundashon pa Planifikashon di Idioma, Willemstad
sources.pages: Phonemic (fonétiko) convention: ⟨k⟩ not ⟨c⟩; digraphs ⟨ch dj sh zh⟩; diacritics ⟨è ò ù ü⟩ for [ɛ ɔ ʏ y]; acute ⟨á é í ó ú⟩ marks stress. Distinct from the Aruban etymological spelling (⟨c qu⟩, closer to Spanish/Portuguese).
sources.notes: Defines the Curaçao/Bonaire phonemic orthography this spec models; the gold register uses this convention, NOT the Aruban etymological one.

sources.author: Maurer, P.
sources.title: L'Angola dans la genèse du Papiamentu
sources.publisher: In: Estudos de Linguística Criola, Casa da Moeda, Lisbon
